channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Rising Consumer Insolvencies in Canada Push More People to Seek Debt Relief Support
item.description: Canada is experiencing a significant rise in consumer debt problems, with more than 37,000 people filing for insolvency during the first three months of 2026, the highest quarterly total since the aft...

item.title: Tinio criticises defence chief over claim linking all Toboso deaths to NPA rebels
item.description: Opposition figure Antonio Tinio has strongly criticised Defence Secretary Gilbert Teodoro Jr. following his statement that all fatalities in Toboso were members of the New People’s Army (NPA). In a re...

item.title: India fertility rate falls to 1.9; only six states remain above replacement level
item.description: India’s fertility pattern continues to show a steady decline, according to the latest Sample Registration System (SRS) report for 2024. The country’s total fertility rate (TFR) has dropped to 1.9 from...

item.title: Immigration Service reassures public over border security amid Ebola concerns
item.description: The Nigeria Immigration Service (NIS) has reassured Nigerians that the country’s land borders are properly secured and not porous, despite growing concerns over the spread of Ebola in parts of Central...

item.title: María Félix: Life and Legacy of a Golden Age Mexican Film Icon
item.description: María Félix (1914–2002) was one of the most influential figures in the Golden Age of Mexican cinema, widely admired for her commanding screen presence, striking beauty, and strong-willed personality. ...

item.title: Canadian activists detained during Gaza aid flotilla return home after deportation from Israel
item.description: Nine of the 12 Canadians who participated in a flotilla attempting to deliver symbolic humanitarian aid to Gaza are expected to return to Canada this weekend after being detained by Israeli forces. Th...

item.title: Man dies after using Kambo frog secretion during detox ceremony in Leicester
item.description: A 40-year-old British man has died after taking part in a so-called detoxification ritual involving Kambo, a toxic secretion derived from the skin of Amazonian tree frogs. The man, Kristian Trend, col...

item.title: Sitti Navarro shares personal experiences about fertility and motherhood
item.description: Filipina singer and bossa nova artist Sitti Navarro recently shared details about her personal fertility journey, giving fans a glimpse into the challenges and realities she experienced while preparin...

item.title: Secret Service fatally shoots armed suspect after gunfire near White House checkpoint
item.description: On Saturday evening, an armed suspect was shot and killed by U.S. Secret Service officers after opening fire near a security checkpoint outside the White House complex in Washington, D.C. The incident...

item.title: Collection of 1920s–1930s Postcards Featuring Actress Anna May Wong
item.description: The article presents a curated collection of 25 vintage postcards featuring Anna May Wong, one of the first Chinese American film stars to achieve international recognition in Hollywood. Born Wong Liu...

item.title: Isaac Smith suggests Tasmania Devils should prioritise Harley Reid over Nick Daicos in recruitment discussion
item.description: AFL premiership player Isaac Smith has weighed into speculation about the future list-building strategy of Tasmania’s incoming AFL expansion side, the Tasmania Devils. The discussion centres on whethe...

item.title: How Alan Peter Cayetano became Senate President amid internal Senate power struggle
item.description: The article details the turbulent and highly political events that led to Senator Alan Peter Cayetano becoming Senate President in May 2026, following a leadership takeover described as a “coup” withi...

item.title: Tinubu Secures Overwhelming Support Across States in APC 2027 Presidential Primaries
item.description: President Bola Tinubu emerged as the clear winner of the All Progressives Congress (APC) presidential primaries held across Nigeria ahead of the 2027 general elections. The exercise took place simulta...

item.title: US and Iran report progress on preliminary agreement amid dispute over Strait of Hormuz terms
item.description: US President Donald Trump has announced that a preliminary agreement with Iran has been “largely negotiated”, though he emphasised that final details still need to be resolved. According to his statem...

item.title: Supporters gather in Edmonton for launch of Forever Canadian campaign office
item.description: A large crowd gathered in Edmonton for the opening of the Forever Canadian campaign office, marking the launch of a new political and community-focused initiative. The event attracted hundreds of supp...

item.title: Suspect killed after firing on Secret Service outside White House; bystander critically injured
item.description: A shooting incident occurred outside the White House in Washington, D.C., when a 21-year-old man allegedly pulled a revolver from a bag and opened fire toward U.S. Secret Service agents. According to ...

item.title: Rob Base, hip-hop artist behind ‘It Takes Two’, dies aged 59
item.description: Rob Base, the American rapper best known for the 1988 hip-hop hit ‘It Takes Two’, has died at the age of 59 following a private battle with cancer. His death was confirmed through a statement shared o...

item.title: White House Gunman Identified After Shooting at Secret Service Checkpoint
item.description: A 21-year-old man identified as Nassir Best was named as the suspect involved in a shooting incident near the White House on May 23, 2026. According to reports cited by MS NOW and Raw Story, Best alle...

item.title: Visually impaired students in Patna receive chocolates during BJP president Nitin Nabin’s birthday observance
item.description: On the occasion of BJP national president Nitin Nabin’s 46th birthday, celebrations were held in Patna on May 23, 2026, in a simple manner involving party leaders and workers. While the leader himself...

item.title: Celtic seal league and Scottish Cup double after beating Dunfermline 3-1
item.description: Celtic completed a successful domestic double after defeating Dunfermline 3-1 in the Scottish Cup final at Hampden Park on Saturday. The victory came only one week after Celtic secured the Scottish Pr...
